feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Echoes of Excellence: Highlights from the Disability:IN Conference & Expo
entry.content:
As SAP’s Autism at Work program marks its 11th year, its impact continues to resonate across industries, inspiring numerous companies to embrace the journey toward autism inclusivity. Through strategic partnerships and committed advocacy, the program has established itself as a leader in promoting a mor inclusive workplace for individuals on the autism spectrum. 

 For the second consecutive year, SAP partnered with Disability:IN to recognize and honor organizations that are making significant strides in promoting autism inclusion. This collaboration led to the creation of the Autism Inclusion Company of the Year Award, which was presented at the Disability:IN Conference & Expo in Las Vegas, Nevada on July 16, 2024.   The Autism Inclusion Company of the Year Award was presented to DXC at the Disability:IN Conference. SAP Autism at Work program played a pivotal role in advising on the award’s selection criteria, while Disability:IN independently reviewed the submissions and selected the winner, ensuring a fair and impactful recognition process. 

SAP’s commitment to inclusivity was further underscored when it was named a Best Place to Work for People with Disabilities in Brazil, the United States, Canada, and Germany in 2024. This accolade reflects our ongoing efforts to expand accessibility for employees and partners around the world, and our accessibility-focused Employee Network Groups are valuable contributors to such practices and culture across SAP. The Disability Equality Index, which evaluates companies across six categories—Culture & Leadership, Enterprise-Wide Access, Employment Practices, Community Engagement, and Supplier Diversity—highlighted SAP's excellence, marking the sixth consecutive year that SAP America achieved a perfect score of 100. 

SAP’s dedication to inclusivity was also evident at the 2024 Global Disability: IN Conference, which offered a platform for sharing insights and best practices on disability inclusion. With over 5,000 attendees participating both in-person and virtually, the conference featured sessions on crucial topics such as self-identification, accessibility, creating inclusive content, AI integration, talent acquisition, and supporting neurodivergent employees.  

 

After a successful event, we gathered some exciting experiences and meaningful reflections from our peers and colleagues.  

Our colleagues, Liliana (Lili) Zolt (she/they), Head of Influencer Strategy for SAP SuccessFactors, Haldis Gonsalves (She/Her), Senior Director, Platform AI Experience & Strategy, and Ezequiel Massa (he/him), Head of Diversity & Inclusion, SAP Latin America & Caribbean, provided compelling testimonies on the profound lessons learned from the conference and offered valuable insights into the key takeaways. To fully appreciate their perspectives, it’s essential to understand their backgrounds. Haldis, Ezequiel, and Liliana’s personal experiences play a crucial role in illustrating how inclusivity can be seamlessly woven into every individual’s journey. By drawing on different perspectives, we can find how lived experiences can be instrumental in fostering a deeper, more authentic approach to inclusivity and inclusive best practices.  

 Let’s dive into Liliana’s story: 

Liliana is the Global Co-Lead for SAP HEAR & Friends: SAP’s employee network group (ENG) for people with hearing loss and their allies. Profoundly Deaf and fluent in both English and American Sign Language (ASL), Lili is a cochlear implant recipient committed to fostering a tight-knit Deaf community within and outside of SAP.  

Lili was one of the attendees of the Disability:IN Global Conference in Las Vegas, Nevada. Over three busy days filled with networking, learning, and fun, Liliana identified key takeaways from the conference: trends within AI, storytelling, and accessibility. “For starters, AI will accelerate inclusivity and create more opportunities for disabled talent in the future of work. Secondly, focusing on intersectional representation in storytelling empowers individuals and strengthens brand relationships. Lastly, organizations that prioritize accessibility from the get-go are ahead of the game, and the future requires organizations to move past individual-use cases and ensure longevity of their brand by setting the standard as access for all”, shared Lili.     

Now, let’s learn more about Ezequiel’s story: 

Ezequiel has been working in the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) space for the past 10 years, considering the time invested in volunteer assignments where he was able to turn his purpose and calling into his full-time role as Head of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion at SAP LAC in 2020. Through his foundation as a results-oriented leader in the tech industry, he is skilled in leading by influence, public speaking, and community engagement.  In 2019, Ezequiel was selected by OUTstanding and Yahoo Finance as one of the “50 LGBT+ Future Leaders”. 

While reflecting on his own experience at the conference, Ezequiel shared, “Attending the Disability:IN Conference is always a powerful learning experience, yet it's not just about the lessons learned if you ask me. What I treasure most of these opportunities is having the chance to connect with fellow SAP colleagues supporting disability inclusion around the globe and hearing about the impressive work other organizations are driving. Being Co-Chair to the Disability:IN LATAM Council for the past three years and in my capacity as Head of D&I for Latin America at SAP, the main highlight for me was seeing SAP Brazil score 100 points on the Disability Equality Index. I'm feeling proud and energized to strengthen our work in the region together with our leaders and Employee Network Groups”. 

 Lastly, let’s look at Haldis’ story:  

Haldis Gonsalves, Senior Director at SAP in Platform AI Experience & Strategy, combines her personal experiences with ASD and ADHD with her professional expertise to champion neurodiversity in the workplace. Since joining SAP in 2020, she has worked on multiple cross-product initiatives, including SAP Unified Analytics, SAP Task Center, SAP Unified Services and BTP Experience Platform Vision and Strategy. Haldis is dedicated to creating a more inclusive tech industry, using AI to improve accessibility and support diverse communities.   

After her time at the event, Haldis shared, "Attending my first Disability:IN Conference this year was both eye-opening and deeply personal. It was validating to see neurodiversity at the forefront of discussions and to connect my AI work with practical applications driving inclusivity. One standout moment was exploring how multimodal GenAI assistants can improve the daily lives of neurodivergent employees, including myself, and empower ERGs to create more inclusive workplaces. A key takeaway that resonated with me was the need to expand job opportunities beyond entry-level roles for neurodivergent talent. This really hit home because it's crucial to show that inclusivity extends beyond just getting in the door—it’s about creating pathways for growth and career advancement, especially for those of us diagnosed later in life. Being part of a conference that truly celebrated and pushed for these changes was inspiring and empowering."

 

The success of the conference and the recognition of SAP as a leader in disability inclusion reflect the company's unwavering commitment to creating a more inclusive workplace for all. As we continue to advance these efforts, we invite you to take an active step towards autism inclusion by signing the Autism Inclusion Pledge. Together, we can build a future where everyone can thrive.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne September '24
entry.content:
Melbourne! we are back again for the 4th time this year. And maybe there is a spring in your step as we head out of winter...

Last meet up was fantastic with a great mix of fresh faces and those familiar regulars. And as always really interesting conversations.

By now you know the drill, and if not check out the previous Stammtische from this year (March, May & June). Essentially, just come along for as long as you like if you can. No need to book or register, just join the fun. The venue has a relaxed vibe, excellent for conversation over a drink and good food (all optional). Reach out if you have any questions though.

The November Melbourne Stammtisch is slated to coincide with the TechEd on Tour event - details to come...

Looking forward to catching you there, and please remember to spread the word around.

Thursday 12th September 2024, 5:30pm onwards at The Bank on Collins 

 Ground floor/394 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000.

 

When you arrive, if you can't find us you can ask for a booking for the "SAP Regulars Table"

entry.title: Bienvenidos al grupo de la comunidad en Madrid 🇪🇸
entry.content:
¡Hola miembros de SAP Community!

Estoy muy emocionado de poder crear este espacio donde los apasionados del mundo SAP podamos conectarnos, compartir experiencias y aprender juntos. Mi nombre es Antonio Maradiaga y soy parte de esta gran empresa que es SAP pero aparte de ello me gusta mucho compartir con miembros de la comunidad SAP.

You don't speak Spanish? Not a problem... There are also English speakers in the group 🙂 Please join us!

La idea de este grupo, es poder tener un espacio donde los que estamos en Madrid 🇪🇸,  apasionados de
todo este mundo de tecnologia SAP podamos conectarnos, compartir experiencias y aprender juntos.

Independientemenete de tu background en el mundo SAP, ya seas un desarrollador, consultor, estudiante, usuario o simplemente alguien concuriosidad por SAP, este grupo está abierto para todos. Pronto estaré organizando quedadas (SAP Stammtisch), sesiones tecnicas (SAP CodeJams) y tambien compartire otro tipo de eventos  que pasen en todo este mundo de SAP en Madrid.

Asi que, unanse al grupo para recibir notificaciones de los eventos que pasen en Madrid. (Make sure to join the group so you can be notified of future events!)

¡Nos vemos pronto!

Antonio Maradiaga

entry.title: 🎙️Episódio 01 - Live Track Podcast: Clean Core com SAP BTP para otimizar processos empresariais
entry.content:
Temos um novo episódio especial no Live Track Podcast, trazendo insights valiosos diretamente da comunidade SAP no Brasil. Este canal é o seu ponto de encontro para acompanhar transmissões ao vivo, gravações e replays dos principais eventos SAP, como o SAP Inside Track. Se você perdeu alguma edição ou quer rever os destaques, este é o lugar certo! E, claro, não deixe de conferir os conteúdos passados, sempre focados em temas que estão moldando o futuro da tecnologia e dos negócios.

Assista ao episódio completo e prepare-se para o que vem por aí no #SITREC2024

🔗 🎙️Live Track Podcast: Clean Core com SAP BTP para otimizar processos empresariais 

 

Live Track Podcast:

Transmitimos ao vivo uma conversa imperdível sobre como o conceito de Clean Core, integrado ao SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), está transformando a otimização dos processos empresariais. Para discutir este tema, tivemos a honra de receber Lucas Ferreira, Senior Account Executive da Tachyonix.

Detalhes do Episódio:

Em uma conversa dinâmica no formato de podcast, nossa equipe apresentou temas que serão abordados no próximo SAP Inside Track 2024 em Recife, no dia 8 de outubro. Lucas Ferreira compartilhou como está ajudando empresas a adotar o conceito de Clean Core em suas implementações SAP S/4HANA gerando uma verdadeira transformação digital. Lucas já trouxe essa apresentação para o SIT São Paulo 2024, onde levantou o público com suas ideias inovadoras. A palestra em Recife promete ser um dos grandes destaques do evento!

Convidados Especiais:

Além de Lucas Ferreira, contamos com a presença de nomes da comunidade SAP, como: @Marssel700 
@afigueredo54 Helder Lins.

Durante a live, discutimos em detalhes os principais temas que serão abordados no SAP Inside Track 2024 em Recife incluindo:

  • Metodologias de otimização com Clean Core
  • Integração com o SAP BTP
  • Casos de sucesso e aplicação prática nas empresas

Assista ao episódio completo e prepare-se para o que vem por aí no #SITREC2024

🔗 🎙️Live Track Podcast: Clean Core com SAP BTP para otimizar processos empresariais 

Se você gostou do conteúdo, aproveite para seguir o canal e ficar por dentro dos próximos lançamentos e eventos!

🔗A agenda completa do evento já foi divulgada.

Te vejo lá!

entry.title: Resolving Job Scheduler Issue After Incentive Management Maintenance
entry.content:
Issue Description

During weekly maintenance window for SAP Success Factors Incentive Manager, jobs in the XS Advanced Job Scheduler service would stop working. The logs for the jobs would show the following error:

As a workaround, the node js application in Web IDE was restarted and the jobs would begin working again.

When an application is run via the Web IDE it stays active as long as the current user session is active or expiration of access token. Afterwards, the application endpoints stop being reachable, and this is the cause of error observed above.

This behavior is avoided by deploying the applications to the XS Advanced Runtime environment via MTAR file. The following steps show how to do this.

 

Fix Steps

Web IDE

  • Login to SAP Web IDE for SAP HANA.
  • Switch to the relevant workspace for the application.
     
  • Go to Development </>.

  • Right-click on the project that contains the application → Build → Build.
  • Once complete, open the project mta_archives.
  • Right-click mtar file → Deploy → Deploy to XS Advanced.

     

  • Properties should be pre-populated to:
    • Organization: orgname.
    • Space: same as database name

  • Click Deploy.
  • Wait for deployment success message:

Job Scheduler

  • Login to the Job Scheduler dashboard.
  • Search Instances
  • Click “Open Dashboard”
  • Click on any job named orgname_****.xsjob
  • Take the URL for “Job Action” up to the last forward slash, it will be in the format "https:// orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com":
  • Open support ticket – edit the bold parts accordingly:

HANA JobScheduler <tenant_id> URL Whitelist

Please can the URL "https://orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com" be added to the hosts file and be locally resolvable for the jobScheduler in <tenant_id> (https://jobscheduler-dashboard.<env>.calliduscloud.com ).

This is to resolve the error:

Response: Request Error: Code:-ENOTFOUND, Message:-getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND orgname-<db_name>-<node_js_name>.<env>.calliduscloud.com

  • Once the ticket has been completed, any jobs not beginning with “orgname” should be deleted from Job Scheduler Dashboard  “jobScheduler – Jobs” area.

entry.title: Empowerment of Women Through Education and Livelihood Skill-Building
entry.content:
Women’s empowerment is a humanitarian choice, and it’s a relatively easy one to make when we prioritize education and genuinely enriching entrepreneurial opportunities. Women’s empowerment doesn’t just improve the lives of the women concerned, however – it is a lucrative way to boost the entire global economy and to help people around the world achieve climate justice for all.

Barefoot College International(BCI) has always recognized the significance of spearheading women-centric and women-run vocational programs. At the grassroots level, women, historically underrepresented and undervalued, are an untapped resource that benefits entire communities when cultivated.

For example, a highly acclaimed McKinsey studyreports that including more women in the workforce could boost global GDP by US$12 trillion by 2025. Narrowing gender disparity has substantial benefits aside from the purely economic, such as providing an increase in planned parenthood, improved sexual health for women and girls, and overall greater awareness and adaptability to the regional effects of climate change.

Statistically, more than 90% of rural women who are offered entrepreneurial and educational resources end up staying in their local villages. Women are more predisposed to return to their communities instead of leaving to monetize their skills elsewhere after training, and they are more likely, too, to share the knowledge they acquire with their friends and family, especially their children – regardless of the child’s gender.

Educational opportunities provide women with not only a chance to acquire skills they would otherwise never have had the chance to learn, but it also vastly improves opportunities for those around them. It also encourages a culture of transferring knowledge and information by witnessing the benefits of education as an income-generating factor that benefits men as well.

Barefoot College International has tailored their initiatives to the leadership potential of women through regionally specific livelihoods and entrepreneurial opportunities. These include programs such as solar engineering, sewing and drone pilot programs, and agricultural livelihood programs, including organic coffee farming and beekeeping. Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEI&B) are at the forefront of the BCI curriculum, including digital literacy, women’s rights, and women’s sexual health programs.

With these comprehensive and holistic educational experiences, BCI’s mission to spearhead economic mobility and autonomy for marginalized communities can be achieved fast and effectively, and most importantly with equity.

To donate to this great organization, visit their website.

Check out the recent research from the SAP SuccessFactors HR research team:

Whitepaper: Four approaches to making skills a reality in your organization

Infographics:

  • Assessing the skills-based hiring timeline
  • Changing mindsets about skills-based hiring
  • Winning the race for skills with skills-based pay

Videos:

  • 4 approaches to making skills a reality in your organization
  • 6 questions you should be asking as you pursue your skills journey
  • Skills-based practices: spotlights on hiring and pay

Blogs:

  • Making Skills a Reality in Your Organization
  • Norsk Hydro Empowers Talent with AI Capabilities on Journey to Become a Skills-Based Organization

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Integrate SAP BTP Cloud Foundry with IBM Instana
entry.content:
How about adding additional monitoring and performance visibility to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) Cloud Foundry applications?
Whether you're working with SAP Ui5/Fiori, Node.js, or Java Spring Boot applications, this guide provides you with interactive steps to easily install the IBM® Instana agent and start monitoring your apps in real time.

Steps:

You need to get a SaaS license offering of IBM Instana for SAP Business Technology Platform. 

Plug & Play for SAP Ui5/Fiori Applications

  1. Insert the Instana Script:
    • Open the index.html of your SAP Ui5 application. It will be inside the webapp directory.


      • Add the following script to enable Instana monitoring: 
        <script>
  (function(s,t,a,n){s[t]||(s[t]=a,n=s[a]=function(){n.q.push(arguments)},
  n.q=[],n.v=2,n.l=1*new Date)})(window,"InstanaEumObject","ineum");
  ineum('reportingUrl', ''); // Add your reporting URL here
  ineum('key', ''); // Add your Instana key here
  ineum('trackSessions');
</script>
<script defer crossorigin="anonymous" src="https://eum.instana.io/1.6.6/eum.min.js" integrity=""></script>
  2. You're All Set:

       Deploy your application, and the Instana agent will begin monitoring immediately.

Plug & Play for Node.js Applications

  1. Install the Instana Package:

    • If using npm, run:
    npm install --save @instana/serverless-collector
    • If using yarn, run:
    yarn add @instana/serverless-collector

  2. Configure the Manifest File:

    • Update your manifest.yaml with the necessary Instana configurations. The manifest location will be at the root directory of the application:

  • applications:
- name: node-demo-api
  host: node-demo-api
  domain: cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com
  buildpacks:
    - nodejs_buildpack # optional
  env:
    NODE_OPTIONS: '--require ./node_modules/@instana/serverless-collector'
    INSTANA_AGENT_KEY: '' # Add your agent key
    INSTANA_ENDPOINT_URL: '' # Add the endpoint URL
    INSTANA_LOG_LEVEL: '' # Set the log level

     

Plug & Play for Java Spring Boot Applications

  1. Download the Instana Collector:

    • Obtain the latest standalone JVM collector from the Instana repository: Instana Collector Download

  2. Update the Manifest File:

    • Add the following configurations to your manifest.yml. The manifest location will be at the root directory of the application:

 

applications:
- name: rest-demo-app
  path: ./target/rest-0.0.1-SNAPSHOT.jar
  host: rest-demo-app
  domain: cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com
  env:
    JBP_CONFIG_JAVA_OPTS: 'java_opts: "-javaagent:BOOT-INF/lib/standalone-collector-jvm-[version].jar"'
    INSTANA_AGENT_KEY: '' # Add your agent key
    INSTANA_ENDPOINT_URL: '' # Add the endpoint URL
    INSTANA_LOG_LEVEL: 'TRACE' # Set the log level

 

Final Steps

Deploy Your Applications: - Push your applications to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry with the Instana agent configurations enabled. For SAP UI5/Fiori apps,i.e., multi target applications:

   mbt build

and then Deploy mta archives.

For other cloud native applications, do a

cf push

Monitor in Real-Time: - Use the Instana dashboard to monitor your applications in real-time, providing you with insights and actionable data to optimize performance and troubleshoot issues. For reference, we are leveraging the REST calls failures here:

By following these interactive steps, you can effortlessly integrate Instana with your SAP Cloud Foundry applications, providing additional monitoring and performance visibility .

Happy coding and monitoring!

entry.title: Experience Report: ABAP Code Retreat 2024 – A Beginner's Perspective
entry.content:
ABAP Code Retreat 2024 at Inwerken AG

On September 10th, 2024, the ABAP Code Retreat took place once again in Isernhagen (Hannover). Originally launched in 2016 by @majcon and @ceedee666 this event gathers ABAP developers to code and learn together. The focus of the retreat was on Test Driven Development (TDD), with challenges like "Game of Life" or the "Bowling Kata" used to demonstrate this methodology. The event at Inwerken AG was organized and coordinated by @EnnoWulff , who ensured everything ran smoothly. As beginners, we would like to share our impressions and experiences from this inspiring workshop.

What were our expectations?

Before the event, we had mixed feelings about the ABAP Code Retreat. On one hand, we were excited to expand our knowledge, on the other, we were concerned about being overwhelmed as newcomers. We were curious to see how developers from other companies tackle problems and expected to be challenged, both by the topic and by engaging with more experienced developers.

How did it turn out?

Our initial concerns quickly disappeared once we received a thorough introduction to Test Driven Development. This helped us understand the goal of the workshop and the importance of the methodology. Thanks to the support from both the other participants and the moderator, we never felt left behind and were able to follow along at a comfortable pace.

Pair Programming was one of the biggest challenges, but also one of the most rewarding experiences. Working as a team was essential, and it helped us organize our thought process around solving problems. The alternating roles were particularly beneficial. While one person coded, the other could think through the next steps. This shift in perspective allowed both partners to contribute in different, valuable ways.

It was also fascinating to see how developers from other companies approached and solved similar challenges. And, of course, it was a great opportunity to make new connections that might extend beyond the workshop itself.

What did we learn?

A key takeaway from the retreat was an introduction to Test Driven Development (TDD). This approach is very different from typical development workflows. Instead of jumping straight into coding, TDD starts with the question: "What should my method produce as the final result?". The first step is writing a unit test that checks the desired output. Only after that do you implement the method. Finally, the unit test is used to verify whether the method's logic is correct or needs refining.

This reverse way of thinking made it much easier to code in a focused manner and spot potential problems early on. It was an "aha" moment to see how continuous testing during development.

Who should attend this workshop?

We recommend this workshop to developers looking to explore new approaches and improve their workflow. It is especially useful for ABAP developers with little to no experience in Test Driven Development (TDD), offering a hands-on opportunity to practice new methodologies that can be easily applied to everyday work.

Additionally, developers seeking to broaden their network and exchange ideas with colleagues from other companies will find this event beneficial. The collaboration during Pair Programming and the sharing of ideas provides a chance to view problems from multiple perspectives.

The ABAP Code Retreat was a valuable experience for us, helping us grow both professionally and personally. We’re looking forward to future events where we can further deepen our knowledge and make new connections.

~Ben

~@l_e_n_a 

~@Hoolya

entry.title: Join SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 to Explore the Latest in SAP Integration Technologies!
entry.content:
Dates: October 8–9, 2024
Event: High-energy, interactive, and accessible virtual experience

Are you interested in SAP integration technologies? Don’t miss the chance to explore SAP Integration Suite's latest advancements at SAP TechEd Virtual 2024! This year’s event features a range of sessions focused on SAP Integration-related topics, providing valuable insights and practical knowledge.

SAP Integration Module Topics & Sessions:

  1. IN100 | Innovate Now with a Modern, AI-Assisted Integration Suite
    Track Overview (45 min)
    Discover how SAP Integration Suite’s key capabilities and recent innovations support scalable, real-time integration of business processes, APIs, and events across SAP and third-party landscapes.
  2. IN103 | Modernize and Elevate to the Cloud with SAP Integration Suite
    Lecture (25 min)
    Learn how to transition smoothly from legacy systems to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite, including migration tools and services for cloud-based integration.
  3. IN101 | Leverage Real-Time Business Insights with an Event-Driven Architecture
    Lecture (25 min)
    Explore SAP’s event-driven ecosystem and advanced event mesh capabilities. Understand how to manage event-driven scenarios to enhance business processes within the integration module.
  4. IN102 | Take Control of Your APIs with the API Management and Graph Capabilities
    Lecture (25 min)
    Learn how to govern and protect your APIs using SAP Integration Suite’s API Management and Graph capabilities. See the latest features and how they fit into your integration strategy.
  5. IN105 | Unleash the Power of AI-Driven Enterprise Automation with SAP Solutions
    Lecture (25 min)
    Discover how AI capabilities combined with SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build, and SAP Signavio can drive automation and process improvement within your integration module.
  6. IN201 | Dive into the B2B World of Integration
    Lecture (25 min)
    Simplify B2B integration scenarios with SAP Integration Suite, accelerate trading partner onboarding, and ensure smooth transactions and data exchange.
  7. IN200 | Unleash the Power of AI in Enterprise Integrations
    Lecture (25 min)
    Explore how SAP Integration Suite leverages AI to enhance enterprise integrations, improve efficiencies, and drive innovation within your integration strategy.
  8. IN106 | Ferring’s Integration Journey from Legacy to SAP Integration Suite
    Lecture (25 min)
    Hear about Ferring’s evolution from basic to advanced integration scenarios with SAP Integration Suite, including B2B implementations and real-time integrations.
  9. IN202 | Gain Flexibility and Scalability with a Hybrid Integration Platform
    Lecture (25 min)
    Learn about Edge Integration Cell, a next-gen integration runtime enabling API management and integration scenarios within private landscapes.
  10. IN104 | Design and Run Your Integrations Smoothly with SAP Integration Suite
    Lecture (25 min)
    Understand how to incorporate design guidelines, monitor integrations with SAP Cloud ALM, and configure alerts for efficient integration management.

Register Now: SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 Registration

Register now to deepen your expertise in the latest SAP integration technologies

entry.title: CIC Support Webcasts
entry.content:
Discover more about CIC support webcasts 

Getting the maximum support from SAP has never been easier! Read on to learn more about the support webcasts on offer by CIC

What are CIC support webcasts? 

CIC support webcasts, which are available to all SAP customers and partners, highlight the key support resources available to you and teach you how to interact successfully with our support organisation. 

If you are unsure of anything during or after the webcast our CIC experts will be on hand to answer any questions you may have via the webcast chat or the Q&A session at the end.  

 

Why sign up for these Support Webcasts? 

By doing so you will become a Po at accessing the key support resources available to you as they teach you how to get the most from your support.  

The support webcasts are offered in a variety of languages such as English, French, Spanish and German, right across different time zones, so there will always be one that is available to suit your needs 

So, what are you waiting for ……………… 

 

Visit the SAP Support Webcast page to REGISTER for one of the next upcoming webcasts! 

Don’t have time to attend one of the live webcast sessions? 

Don’t worry as you can watch one of the recorded sessions by clicking here 

 

A sneak peek of some of the CIC support webcasts available. 

Case Management 

Description: 

Join this support webcast to learn more about the Get Support application including the steps to report and manage your case.  

Our team will walk you through the elements to help you improve the quality of the case being created and generally enhance your support experience with SAP. The aim is that you will understand the SAP processes that you are expected to follow to get the best results from your SAP engagement.   

Learning Objectives:

Best practices for case creation and enabling you on how to navigate and get the best support option from the Get Support Application 

 

Getting the Most From Your Support 

Description: 

In this webinar you will learn about the Support Portal Resources that are available to SAP Customers.  

Learning objective: 

The objectives are to provide the information needed to successfully engage with our support Organization. 

 

Solution Search and Self-Service Enablement 

Description:

In this session you will learn how to search for known issues and resolutions in all of SAP's knowledge repositories. 

Learning Objectives: 

Become more familiar with SAP Support's self-enablement tools and the different ways to find a solution for your issue before a case is needed. 

 

Managing your SAP Universal ID 

Description:

Join this support webcast to learn more about SAP Universal Id, how to create and manage your profile and much more. Our team will walk you through the creation process as well as linking accounts to your profile. We will also cover some common scenarios that customer encounter, to equip you with information in case you face the same issues.  

Learning Objectives: 

Best practices for SAP Universal ID management, enabling you to manage your UID including on how to create, unlinking a user ID, handling email address changes, and more. But that's not all – we understand that recent changes to receiving and transferring certifications on the SAP Training Portal and SAP Learning Hub may have left you with questions. That's why we'll also provide guidance on these changes, ensuring that you have all the information you need to navigate these updates confidently. 

 

Thanks for reading and we look forward to seeing you in our upcoming webcasts!  

   

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have.   

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community   

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: 🚀 Welcome to the SAP Community Group – Pune! 🚀
entry.content:
Hello SAP Enthusiasts,

I’m Punith Oswal, one of the facilitators of SAP Community Group for Pune, and it gives me immense pleasure to welcome each one of you to this vibrant and thriving community. Whether you're a seasoned professional, a student, or someone just starting your SAP journey, this group is designed to be a collaborative space where we can all grow together.

### Why Join?
The SAP ecosystem is evolving rapidly, and Pune has emerged as a hub for innovation, development, and knowledge-sharing in SAP technologies. This community is a place for all of us to exchange ideas, discuss the latest trends, and network with like-minded individuals who are as passionate about SAP as you are!

### What to Expect:
- Knowledge Sharing: We’ll host regular discussions on SAP-related topics—be it integration (SAP CPI), SAP S/4HANA, analytics, cloud solutions, or under the SAP umbrella.

- Events & Webinars: Look forward to insightful webinars, workshops, and local meetups, where we can dive deep into technical sessions, case studies, and new SAP releases. Don't forget to mark your calendar for special events like *The Future of Integration 2024* happening soon!

- Networking Opportunities: This is a fantastic platform to connect with peers, SAP experts, and industry leaders within the Pune region. You can share your experiences, ask questions, share knowledge and build lasting professional relationships.

- Career Growth: Learn from experts, gain insights into different SAP modules, and enhance your career by staying updated with the latest SAP certifications, projects, and events in Pune.

### How to Participate:
- Ask Questions: No question is too small or too complex! If you're working on a tricky issue or have queries, feel free to post. Someone here will have an answer!

- Share Experiences: We want to hear from you! Whether it's a new project, success story, or challenge you're facing, sharing can help others and foster mutual growth.

- Be Engaged: Participate in polls, join discussions, attend events, and offer your insights. This community thrives when everyone contributes.

Let’s come together to build a strong, dynamic, and inclusive SAP community that empowers every member to succeed. Feel free to introduce yourself in the comments below, and let's get the conversations rolling!

I am also joined by my fellow Pune community members @saicharank @Devkambli @Pari1 @Asutosh21 who are co-running this group with me and helping organize these exciting events. Together, we aim to create an environment where everyone can learn, share, and grow. 

Here’s to learning, growing, and leading together! 🌟

Welcome aboard, and let’s make this journey unforgettable!

 

Warm regards,
Punith Oswal
SAP Community Group - Pune

entry.title: SAP TechEd Virtual & TechEd on Tour (SAUG-x)
entry.content:
SAP TechEd Virtual 2024 is scheduled for Oct 8 & 9. The session catalog has been released and its time to build your agenda. This is available for all and a fantastic opportunity to learn new things.

Check out the session catalog https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

For those who are planning to be at Melbourne, SAUG & SAP are partnering to present an in-person experience for the Developer community. This very special gathering in Melbourne full of unique sessions for in-depth learning, hands-on labs to develop practical skills and deep insights to give you an edge. Draft agenda is out. https://www.saug.com.au/WhatsOn/SAUG-x2024

See you there.

entry.title: 🌟 Bem-vindos à nossa Comunidade SAP em Curitiba!
entry.content:
Sejam Bem-vindos a nossa comunidade SAP em Curitiba!

Meu nome é Luiz Carlos de Souza, sou de São Paulo porém resido em Curitiba desde 2021, viemos para passar uma temporada em família e nunca mais retornamos, esta cidade é incrível !

Consultor SAP desde 2004, comecei como desenvolvedor ABAP, migrei em 2013 para a área funcional de seguros e atualmente me encontro focado em Arquitetura Cloud com o Business Technology Platform (BTP) para acompanhar toda essa transformação digital. 

Como curador deste grupo, meu objetivo é unir a nossa comunidade local, compartilhando novidades sobre as últimas soluções SAP, promovendo conexões profissionais através de eventuais encontros, e gerando assim um ótimo networking! 

Estou muito animado em começar essa jornada com vocês, e a sua presença é fundamental para construirmos uma comunidade forte e unida. E quem sabe um dia conseguimos ter no nosso próprio SAP Inside Track. (chega de pegar a BR para ir a São Paulo rs....)   

Convido você a deixar um comentário falando um pouco sobre você, sua experiência e o bairro em que mora, ficarei honrado em te conhecer!

Abraços!

entry.title: 🎙️Episódio 02 - Live Track: Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence para Gestão de Crédito
entry.content:
Neste episódio 2 do Live Track podcast tivemos a honra de entrevistar @Jonys_Arcanjo , consultor SAP SAC/DI do Grupo Aço Cearense. Jonys apresentou uma palestra inspiradora no SAP Inside Track 2024 em Recife, abordando como a Inteligência Artificial, em conjunto com o SAP Data Intelligence, está revolucionando a gestão de crédito no setor industrial. Durante o podcast, ele compartilhou insights valiosos sobre a aplicação de IA para prever riscos, otimizar processos e fortalecer a tomada de decisões nas empresas.

🔗🎙️Live Track Podcast: Inteligência Artificial e SAP Data Intelligence para Gestão de Crédito

A conversa foi mediada por um time de peso: @Marssel700 @MRobalinho @afigueredo54. Além de explorar os pontos centrais da palestra, discutimos com Jonys como a tecnologia de ponta está moldando o futuro da indústria e o papel do SAP nesse contexto. A troca de ideias foi enriquecida pelas experiências práticas de Jonys, que destacou o impacto direto dessas inovações no dia a dia de grandes empresas como o Grupo Aço Cearense.

Não deixe de conferir essa entrevista imperdível e acompanhar todos os episódios do nosso podcast! E se quiser saber mais sobre as palestras e os eventos do SAP Inside Track 2024, acesse os links abaixo para se manter atualizado com tudo o que está rolando.

Reveja o Episódio 1

Se você gostou do conteúdo, aproveite para seguir o canal e ficar por dentro dos próximos lançamentos e eventos!

A agenda completa do evento já foi divulgada.

Te vejo lá!

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, September 20th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, September 23rd.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the first week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt will be posted here in week 3. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  We are kicking things off there with the ever-popular Petoberfest. 

List of activities for contest week 1: 

DAYBADGE CODECONTENT WEEK POINTS
 Devtoberfest 2024 ParticipantRegister200
Monday#5E1C26Devtoberfest 2024 - ABAP, Week 1, Sept. 231200
Monday#D2EAF1Learn about the ABAP Data Dictionary150
Monday#BA7CC8Create an ABAP Database Table and Relevant ABAP Dictionary Objects750
Monday#1B0B12Create Database Table and Generate UI Service200
Monday#725772Devtoberfest 2024 - CAP, Week 1, Sept. 231200
Monday#94918CCreate a CAP Business Service with Node.js Using Visual Studio Code500
Monday#FAC0AADeploy a CAP Business Service to SAP Business Technology Platform300
Tuesday#2B2924Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Week 1, Sept. 241200
Tuesday#D88A4DSAP Crossword1800
Tuesday#A5E5FBCreate a Composite Component (List with Rating)200
Tuesday#083676Create a Social Media Backend150
Tuesday#111D2ACreate a Product List Page with Ratings150
Tuesday#433433Prepare a Product Page150
Tuesday#2A170ACreate the Product Page UI with Ratings/Comments400
Wednesday#FEFEFFDevtoberfest 2024 - Integration, Week 1, Sept. 251200
Wednesday#676F49Set Up Prerequisites for your SAP Integration Suite & SAP Build Apps Mission200
Wednesday#5D219AConsume a Stripe service from SAP Open Connectors and SAP Cloud Integration to create payment transactions100
Wednesday#464471Save, Filter and Get your needed data in your Integration Flow100
Wednesday#D7D8D8Consume a SAP Sales and Service Core API to create Sales Orders using SAP Cloud Integration300
Wednesday#369A33Send SMS using SAP Cloud Integration while consuming a Twilio API100
Wednesday#B4ABA9Configure SAP API Management policies to avoid CORS issues and generate an API Key200
Thursday#EDBABCDevtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data), Week 1 - Sep. 261200
Thursday#190B04Use Trial to Set Up Account for Document Information Extraction and Go to Application50
Thursday#413E44Use Trial to Extract Information from Custom Documents with Generative AI and Document Information Extraction250
Friday#85A969Devtoberfest 2024 - Frontend, Week 1 - Sep. 271200
Friday#228E80Get Ready for UI5-Development on Your Local Machine50
Friday#8F5D85Create an SAPUI5 App from the Command Line150
Friday Devtoberfest 2024 - Petoberfest1000

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in Germany 2024-09 Recap
entry.content:
In mid-September, we held two SAP CodeJam events in Germany: in Isernhagen and Stuttgart. The events focused on getting started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python.

The first one was kindly hosted by Inwerken, and it was great to catch up with @EnnoWulff, who took this picture of me below...

...inviting participants to join our free online Devtoberfest event starting on September 23: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-welcome/ba-p/9358

Then on the way to Stuttgart I had a chance to stop by our SAP headquarters in Walldorf...

...where I had a chance to meet with @dinah for the first time in-real-life...

...and to play with some cool technology.

The second event was kindly hosted by Bosch Digital in Stuttgart and I would like to thank Gamze Öztürk and @AjayKulkarni for the effort to organize it!

And it is always a pleasure to catch up with @cyclingfisch_ with whom we had our micro SAP Stammtisch 🍻

Now, the next stops are in Lisbon on September 25th hosted by SAP Portugal: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-lisboa-pt/ev-p/13773843 and in Porto on September 27 hosted by SAP Inside Track Porto: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-porto-pt/ev-p/13770170 

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-using-sap-hana-quot-as-a-new-sap/ba-p/13574098

entry.title: 𝐒𝐀𝐏 𝐈𝐧𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐞 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐜𝐤 𝐆𝐮𝐫𝐮𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 2024: A Remarkable Experience
entry.content:
The morning of SAP Inside Track, Gurugram

As one of the core team members of the much-anticipated SAP Inside Track, Gurugram 2024 event, I woke up to the sound of rainfall still pattering against my balcony. The city was already drenched with heavy rainfall the previous day, and the forecast wasn’t looking any better for today either. My heart raced with a mix of anxiety and excitement. Would the weather dampen the spirits of our attendees? Would all our meticulous planning go in vain? Despite these worries, I knew the SIT event would be a success. As I arrived at the venue, I met the volunteers along with my fellow core team members Vipul Khullar, Vartika Gupta, Anuradha Dhingan, Meghna Patel and Neeraj Manocha. The energy among us was palpable, and as the first guests started to trickle in, we felt a surge of optimism. And there we knew it was going to be a day to remember!

 

 

Kick-off         

The event kicked off with an inspiring welcome note by Aseem Khera, Location Head at SAP Labs India, Gurugram. He underscored the pivotal role of the SAP community in driving innovation and fostering collaboration, emphasizing how events like these create invaluable synergies. Aseem also highlighted SAP’s strategic focus on leveraging artificial intelligence to deliver enhanced value to its customers.

 

This was followed by an inspiring video keynote delivered by Sindhu Gangadharan, Managing Director of SAP Labs India, Head of Customer Innovation Services, and Chairperson of NASSCOM. She emphasized the significance of learning and networking through community events such as SAP Inside Track. Sindhu discussed the substantial impact of artificial intelligence on the global economy and highlighted SAP's pivotal role in assisting businesses to harness the extensive economic advantages of it. Furthermore, she encouraged community members to aspire to become SAP Champions and SAP Mentors.

 

This was succeeded by a keynote presentation titled “Identifying Value-Based Business AI Scenarios with a Human-Centric Approach,” delivered by Kamalika Bhar of our sponsor Mindset Consulting and Divya Chandrika Mohan from SAP.

 

The morning track was successfully concluded with a presentation on SAP Business AI by Sudhir Verma, the Head of Cross Technology & Architecture for Private Cloud Products at SAP. During this session, Sudhir offered valuable insights into the transformative potential of Generative AI, highlighting key innovations such as Joule and the GenAI Hub. Additionally, Neha Anand from SAP showcased various productive use cases utilizing Joule, further enriching the discussion.

 

I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to our host, Vartika Gupta, for expertly keeping the energy and momentum alive!

 

Following, track 1, several sessions were conducted throughout the day, concentrating on SAP Generative AI, SAP Business AI, and a variety of practical use cases across Tracks 2 to 4. Presenters from SAP Labs India, Mindset, IBM, and EY provided valuable insights into how Generative AI within SAP applications is enhancing value for both businesses and customers.

 

Engaging Sessions: Learning and Networking

Track 2

  1. Unleashing the power of Retrieval Augmented Generation with SAP AI Core Generative AI Hub and SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine

     Speaker: Deep Srivastava

 

 

2. SAP Gen AI solutions that won hearts in SAP Hack2Build 2024

   Speaker: Aayush Dhawan

 

3. SAP AI Business Services - Al models, pre-trained on business relevant data

Speaker: Anuradha Dhingan

 

 4. Building an Application with SAP Build Code and Joule

Speaker: Ravi Grover

 

5. SAP Business AI for A Better Gurugram

Speaker: Niladri Bihari Nayak & Haritosh Srivastav

 

Track 3

1. Umbrella of SAP BTP AI

Speaker:  Chandra Bhushan Singh & Mayank Singhal

 

2. Building a Data fabric and Unlocking Business Value with AI in SAP Datasphere

Speaker: Akhil Das & Pratik Doshi

 

3. From Insights to Experience with Business AI in SAP Digital Supply Chain

Speaker: Raunak Agarwal & Shreyance Parakh

 

4. CX AI Toolkit

Speaker: Aditi Dahal, Nikkita & Siddharth Agarwal

 

5. Empower Your Business with Intelligent Situation Handling and Joule

Speaker: Ranjeet Kumar

 

6. SAP Business AI - Innovations in Procurement & Supply Chain

Speaker: Rakesh Pupneja

 

Track 4 was dedicated to Hands on experience activities:

Participants had the opportunity to explore SAP's newest AI features and capabilities through hands-on experience. During the interactive sessions, participants created close to 50 SAP-based applications, showcasing how AI can be practically applied in various business scenarios.

 

Sponsors

Thanks to our esteemed sponsors Deloitte and Mindset and for their contribution in making SAP Inside Track Gurugram 2024 - Business AI Edition a memorable event. 

A Day to Remember: Summary and Reflections

In conclusion, the SAP Inside Track (SIT) was not merely an event; it represented a convergence of shared knowledge, networking opportunities, and collaborative learning within the SAP Community. Over 100 plus community members participated, providing valuable constructive feedback on the overall experience.

 

Throughout the day, I had the privilege of meeting remarkable individuals, and I eagerly anticipate the upcoming SAP Inside Tracks in Gurugram to reconnect with them. The energy and enthusiasm were palpable, a testament to the collective passion driving us forward. I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to all the fellow organizers, volunteers, speakers, and our sponsors, as their contributions were instrumental in the event's success. Thank you once again for your exceptional efforts and commitment. I look forward to collaborating with you all on future endeavours.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling (SAP Build) Sessions
entry.content:
Today is the first day of Devoberfest!!

Make sure you partake of the:

  • Contest -- grand prize is a trip to an SAP TechEd event with a lot of extras; and even though it's several years' old I still love the gameboard
  • Petoberfest – post a picture of your pet 🐢
  • Sessions – find all the sessions in the event calendar 
  • 👉 The annual SAP Crossword Puzzle – if you have the skill, you can earn 1800 points!

Devtoberfest 2024 sessions

Here's a quick rundown of the SAP Build "Tooling Tuesday" sessions, which touch on the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone
  • SAP Build Code
  • MDK
  • S/4HANA + extensions
  • Integration Suite, including event mesh
  • CAP
  • AI Core
  • GenAI + Joule
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management

That's just one day of Devtoberfest!! In each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions directly to the presenter, who will answer them live or in the chat.

September 24

  • 🟣 Transform Data with Scripting in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with SAP Build Code

  • 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro

  • 🟣 Real-World S/4HANA Invoice Approval Process with SAP Build Process Automation

October 1

  • 🟣 SAP Build Apps 2049: Soon with UI5 and GenAI?

  • 🟣 Enable Work Zone to Consume Business Solutions from Other BTP Subaccounts (content federation)

  • 🟣 Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK

  • 🟣 Putting It All Together: S/4HANA Retail Pricing App Using SAP Build, AI, Event Mesh and More

October 15

  • 🟣 S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard

  • 🟣 Guided Experiences with SAP Build Process Automation and Work Zone

  • 🟣 Develop Even Faster (!) with SAP Build Apps Templates

  • 🟣 Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

October 22

  • 🟣 Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps

  • 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects

  • 🟣 Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Extend Joule with Your Own Skills

Learn a lot about SAP development tools, have a lot of fun – and maybe we'll see you at an SAP TechEd event next year 😸

entry.title: Addressing UNSDGs Through Women-Centric Education
entry.content:
As Global Goals Week kicks off at the United Nations Headquarters in New York, Barefoot College International (BCI) reflects on their pledge to address the Sustainability Development Goals (SDGs). Their vocational and livelihood programs holistically offer solutions to 15 of the 17 goals. In the spirit of “leaving no one behind,” BCI focuses their programmatic responses on the accessibility of education and resources for marginalized women.

Overall, it’s because of this women-centric approachthat 15 of the 17 goals are naturally addressed; women and girls of the Global South are often the last to receive educational and entrepreneurial resources. Thus, uplifting women accelerates global prosperity, peace, and Climate Justice, which are key components of the global goals.

SDG #1: No Poverty

Communities and societies are only as strong as their weakest link. Women, the most untapped resource in impoverished villages, have great potential when included in the workforce. Women are statistically responsible for more than three times as much domestic unpaid labor within the family unit. Offering women education, leading to better entrepreneurial and livelihood options locally, increases household incomes, resulting in a higher quality of life.

SDG #4: Quality Education

Offering women and girls educational resources not only provides them with chances at better-paid income jobs, but it also imparts valuable information on their health, including sexual health, planned parenthood, women’s rights, and nutrition. This increases living standards, improves sexual health, lowers the birth rate, and assists women with knowing their legal rights.

SDG #5: Gender Equality

Gender inequality stagnates social progress, due to the loss of potential workforce professionals when women are not included. BCI’s primary mission is to create lasting change from the ground up through harnessing the power of rural communities and their women. This includes ensuring that the next generation is motivated to keep girl children in school, creating a ripple effect of systemic change toward gender-balanced development.

SDG #10: Reduced Inequalities

When we tie poverty to gender inequality, focusing on the education of women is automatically a means to foster a more equitable playing field for rural communities. Often lagging behind their urban neighbors, remote regions have had an uphill battle in maintaining healthy literacy rates and digital savviness. This can be attributed to a lack of accessibility, thus offering these regions the tools to gain experience and knowledge helps them catch up and thrive.

SDG #17: Partnerships for the Goals

BCI has recognized the value of meaningful, lucrative partnerships with entities whose missions align with theirs. To achieve the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UNSDGs) in a timely manner globally, partnering with like-minded organizations is essential.

Call to action

To join forces with BCI, or to support their ongoing work focused on women, education, and sustainability, please visit their website, www.barefootcollege.org.

Also, be sure to check out the latest research from the SAP SuccessFactors HR research team and learn more about SAP’s “zero emissions, zero waste, and zero inequality” approach to sustainability.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Pune (October 2024)
entry.content:
Following the success of this year's Stammtisch 2024, we are excited to announce the inaugural SAP Inside Track Pune! Mark your calendars for October 19, 2024, as we gather SAP enthusiasts, experts, and professionals for a day filled with insightful sessions, networking, and innovation. Registration is now open, and we encourage you to reserve your spot for this exciting event!

What is SAP Inside Track?   
SAP Inside Track is a community-driven event where SAP professionals, developers, consultants, architects, and leaders come together to share their knowledge and insights on various SAP-related topics. This grassroots initiative, organized by members of the SAP Community, provides a platform for fostering connections, staying updated on the latest SAP technologies, and gaining recognition for your technical expertise. 
Know more about SAP Inside Track Here!

Event Highlights

  1. Expert-Led Sessions
  • Keynote Speeches: Hear from industry leaders and SAP experts about the latest advancements in SAP technologies.
  • Technical Deep Dives: Participate in sessions that cover a wide range of topics, from SAP S/4HANA to SAP Cloud Platform, and everything in between.
  • Hands-On Workshops: Engage in interactive workshops designed to provide practical experience with SAP tools and solutions.
  1. Networking Opportunities
  • Meet the Experts: Connect with SAP professionals, developers, and consultants from various industries.
  • Community Building: Build relationships with peers who share your passion for SAP and technology.

Why Attend?

  • Expand Your Knowledge: Gain insights into the latest SAP technologies and trends.
  • Enhance Your Skills: Participate in hands-on workshops and technical sessions.
  • Grow Your Network: Connect with like-minded professionals and industry experts.
  • Get Inspired: Discover new ideas and innovative solutions that can transform your business.

Registration Details

Registration for SAP Inside Track Pune is now open! Don’t miss this opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and stay ahead in the ever-evolving world of SAP. 

Visit the Pune SIT Registration Page: SAP Inside Track (SIT) 2024

Note: This is an in-person event only.

Event Details

  • Date: October 19th, 2024
  • Venue: SAP Labs India, ITPP, Pune
  • Contact: For any queries, please contact us: sappunecommunity@gmail.com

If you want to be a Speaker check this out: Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track Pune 2024... - SAP Community

Join the Conversation

Stay connected and join the conversation on social media using the hashtag #sitPUNE.

We can't wait to see you at SAP Inside Track Pune 2024! Join us as we come together to learn, share, and innovate.

Thanks & Regards

SAP Community Pune Core Team
